frameworkName: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard Version 4.0
applicability: All entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data or sensitive authentication data, including merchants, payment processors, acquirers, issuers, and service providers

controls.title: Network Security Controls
controls.description: Network security controls must be installed and maintained to protect the cardholder data environment, including firewalls, routers, and network segmentation isolating the CDE from untrusted networks.
requiredEvidence: Network segmentation architecture isolating CDE documented in SDS Architecture Overview and Section 1.1 Architecture Diagram
Firewall rules and network access controls described in SDS Security Architecture section
CDE boundary and scope documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Secure Configuration Standards
controls.description: All system components must be configured securely, with vendor default credentials changed, unnecessary services disabled, and a configuration hardening standard applied before deployment.
requiredEvidence: Secure configuration and hardening requirements documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Technology stack with version and hardening references documented in SDS Component Specifications section
Configuration management process referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Cardholder Data Protection
controls.description: Stored cardholder data must be protected using strong cryptography. Primary Account Numbers must be rendered unreadable in storage using tokenisation, truncation, or encryption. Sensitive authentication data must never be stored after authorisation.
requiredEvidence: Cardholder data protection strategy (tokenisation, encryption, truncation) documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
PAN storage and handling rules documented in SDS Database Schema section
Sensitive authentication data prohibition documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Data Transmission Encryption
controls.description: Cardholder data transmitted over open public networks must be encrypted using strong cryptography including TLS 1.2 or higher. Weak protocols must be disabled.
requiredEvidence: TLS version requirements and cipher suite standards documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
API endpoint HTTPS enforcement documented in SDS API Contracts section
Weak protocol prohibition documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Malware Protection
controls.description: All system components must be protected against malware including anti-malware solutions on all applicable systems with regular definition updates and scanning.
requiredEvidence: Malware protection requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Anti-malware controls referenced in SDS Security Architecture section
Endpoint protection strategy documented in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Secure Development and Vulnerability Management
controls.description: All software and system components must be developed securely following a formal SSDLC. Vulnerabilities must be identified and remediated within defined timeframes based on risk rating.
requiredEvidence: Secure SDLC and OWASP Top 10 compliance documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Vulnerability management and patch timelines referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Code review and security testing requirements documented in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Restrict Access to Cardholder Data
controls.description: Access to system components and cardholder data must be restricted to only individuals whose job requires such access, enforced through a need-to-know access control policy.
requiredEvidence: Least-privilege and need-to-know access policy documented in SDS Security Architecture — Authorization subsection
Role definitions and CDE access restrictions described in SDS Component Specifications section
Access control requirements documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)

controls.title: User Authentication and Access Management
controls.description: Individual user accounts must be assigned to all users accessing system components. Multi-factor authentication must be enforced for all access into the CDE and for all non-console administrative access.
requiredEvidence: MFA requirement for CDE and admin access documented in SDS Security Architecture — Authentication subsection
User account management policy referenced in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Password policy and account lifecycle documented in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Physical Access Restrictions
controls.description: Physical access to system components storing, processing, or transmitting cardholder data must be restricted and controlled with visitor logs, physical access controls, and media destruction procedures.
requiredEvidence: Physical access controls for hosting environment referenced in SDS Integration Points section
Media handling and destruction procedures documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Hosting provider physical security attestation referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Logging, Monitoring and Audit Trails
controls.description: All access to network resources and cardholder data must be logged. Logs must be protected from modification and reviewed daily. Audit log retention must cover at least 12 months.
requiredEvidence: Audit logging requirements with 12-month retention documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Audit logging component described in SDS Security Architecture — Audit Logging subsection
SIEM or log aggregation integration referenced in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Security Testing and Vulnerability Scanning
controls.description: System vulnerabilities must be identified through regular scanning and penetration testing. Internal and external scans must be conducted quarterly and after significant changes. Penetration testing must be performed at least annually.
requiredEvidence: Vulnerability scanning and penetration testing requirements documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)
Security testing frequency and scope documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Vulnerability remediation process referenced in SDS Security Architecture section

controls.title: Security Policy and Risk Management
controls.description: An organisational information security policy must address all PCI DSS requirements. An annual risk assessment must be conducted to identify threats and vulnerabilities.
requiredEvidence: Information security policy compliance documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) referencing PCI DSS v4.0
Annual risk assessment requirement referenced in SRS constraint requirements
Security awareness and training programme referenced in SDS Security Architecture section
